content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree of NNN nodes rooted at node 111. Each node uuu initially has a positive value aua_uau​ associated with it.

You randomly choose one node in the tree and change its value to zero. If any node uuu has a value equal to 000, a random node vvv will be chosen in the subtree of uuu (u≠vu \neq vu=v), and the values aua_uau​ and ava_vav​ will be swapped, hence the value of the node vvv will become zero and the process will continue until the zero is on a leaf node. At this point, we will call the tree a final tree.

**Note**: there is exactly one node with a value equal to zero in a final tree and that node is a leaf node.

Your task is to tell how many different final trees are possible. Two final trees are different if there exists at least one node uuu, such that the value of the node uuu in one final tree differs from the value of the node uuu in the other final tree. Since the answer can be large, print it modulo 109+710^9 +7109+7.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the number of nodes.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two integers uuu and vvv, denoting that there is an undirected edge between the node uuu and the node vvv.
The next line contains NNN integers a1,…,aNa_1,\ldots, a_Na1​,…,aN​.
###Output
For each test case, output on a new line, number of different final trees possible, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

###Constraints


1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤N≤1051 \leq N \leq 10^51≤N≤105
1≤u,v≤N1 \leq u, v \leq N1≤u,v≤N
1≤ai≤1091\le a_i\le 10^91≤ai​≤109
The edges form a tree structure.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.
###Sample Input

`3
1
1
4
1 2
2 3
2 4
1 1 1 2
7
1 2
1 3
2 4
2 5
3 6
3 7
1 2 3 4 1 2 1
`###Sample Output

`1
4
14
`###Explanation



For the first test case, the only possible final tree is when node 111 is chosen and its value becomes 0.




For the second test case, the four possible final trees are:



[ 1, 1, 1, 0 ]
[ 1, 1, 0, 2 ]
[ 1, 2, 1, 0 ]
[ 2, 1, 1, 0 ]

( The iii-th number in the array denotes the value of node iii in the final tree).
